text:
what is art? my old socks hanging on the radiator to dry. the ball of yarn my sister is meticulously transforming into a sweater. the polyurethane plastic cover over the table. the 15$ amazon sunset lamp that lights up our living room. the fridge which for some reason is in the living room cause the kitchen in this london apartment is too small. the mold in the cupboard, the PVC flooring with fake wood grain, and the twenty five hundred dollar laptop my dad bought me so i can find a job. art is everywhere around you, you just have to look.

art isnt confined in museum corridors or pretentious high-class galleries. art exists in the tension between you and the world, in the story woven fabric of existence you inhabit. artfulness in life is available to all those who are willing to look with attentive eyes. look around. tell those stories. tell them to yourself. tell them to your friends. find humour and comfort in the realisation of the wild unfolding of your life.

be brave, reach for that notepad, that sketchbook, the forgotten dusty guitar in the corner of your room. turn those stories into flowers, turn the pain into a message in the bottle for your family and your friends to find. send it out into the ether, fear no judgement and you will find yourself, not just a witness, but a co-author of the future.

text:
hey! i am designing a peer-to-peer networking app that uses AI to semantically match people. your phone exchanges digital "pheromones" based on your answers to a survey + bio with nearby phones (200m/500ft)

!NO SWIPING!

the app only works if you're already out and about. you HAVE to go out and serendipitously stumble into a very interesting stranger!

i am imagining this mostly being used in events to begin with. 

would you use something like this? why/why not?

other features include:
- serendipity meter: is the space you're in right now a good "vibematch" for you
- conversation prompts: the algorithm might give u slight hints as to why you were matched and let u figure out your shared interests naturally

text: i say yes. the question is, what does that rulebook look like, and how does it and the structure come into existence in order for the structure to be conscious? how does maximising collective consciousness as a goal for society shape our metaethics and institutions?

text: i can't quite put it into words, but every night, I go to sleep and in the moments before I wake up I feel my brain reconfiguring itself. i feel better, happier. i'm not putting in any effort. i just sleep when i want. i take naps. my sleeping self has solved all my problems.

raw.full_text: i can't quite put it into words, but every night, I go to sleep and in the moments before I wake up I feel my brain reconfiguring itself. i feel better, happier. i'm not putting in any effort. i just sleep when i want. i take naps. my sleeping self has solved all my problems.

text: after I wake up - stillness. *poof* a whole magical reality collapses into itself, leaving behind the negative space where it used to be, and here I am, journaling on twitter, trying to reverse engineer what my unconscious already knows and put it into action.

can we democratically fund artists in tpot? can the scene invest into itself? can patrons, talent, and audience coordinate and flourish? I have toyed with the idea of organising Emergent Grants.

here's how:

text:
we set up two prediction markets:
1) "Who will be the recipient of the next grant?" (anyone can add names)
2) "How much money should they receive?"

and then we set up an open collective collecting donations.

we set up two prediction markets:

text:
thinking about the order of linguistic development of interrogative words lately—as one does. 'what', 'how', 'when', and 'why' are INCREDIBLY POWERFUL SPELLS?!

language is the only way to transmit the subjective unconscious, it hilariously tries to perform a dimensional reduction of our mind into a one dimensional time series

BUT, our language shapes our consciousness so much, and languages carry 100.000 years old memetic garbage

SO—to the point—interrogative words

I'll start in reverse chronological order with 'why' - 'why' implies 1) causality is universal???? 2) human rational faculties can accurately piece together why something likely non-deterministic happened, lmao, 

'when'—Statements dreamed up by the utterly deranged—wdym 13:30 on Wednesday?—btw look up the McKenna-Meyer Goddess Calendar— there is only NOW, things can only be *simultaneous* or *not simultaneous*, light clocks are propaganda served to you by Einstein's Big Clock to sell more train tickets. possibly 'before' and 'after' exist although I'm not sure if those two are the same yet, anyway,

'how'—LET ME FUCKING SLEEP AT NIGHT BRO, I DON'T NEED TO KNOW HOW, NO-ONE DOES, things just happen, and then they are over, "and how would you like your steak cooked sir?" GO FUCK YOURSELF just bring me the food and let me eat in peace and hopefully choke on it

'what'—this bastard started it all—'what is this' GO FUCK YOURSELF YOU PIECE OF SHIT GARBAGE MIND VIRUS FROM THE FUTURE LET ME FEEL THE RAIN ON MY SKIN unfortunately evolution developed meat based boundary defining engines with legs and now we are bootloading the singularity

ANYWAY, in other news, quitting smoking is going well, I'm fine, I'M COMPLETELY FINE, I'm just angry at the OS and thought i'd make some art, I'm going to go be non-verbal in the bathtub now bye

Looks really cool! 

reason for no questions is cause the idea is still not clear in my head and that this is mainly a joke, but one day I'd like to actually raise for something like this.

the idea is a collaborative production platform, so not really social media

think what would happen if obsidian + google docs + twitter (with circles) + version control all had a baby that had very good UI. Maybe throw in a browser and an AI assistant.

I'm just basically annoyed with the friction related to  the app I use every day to communicate with the world, think and learn being distributed amongst 500 different apps, but I am not even sure if addressing this friction is a good thing.

text:
I don't believe in the narrative behind the word "I" anymore. I still believe I exist, and that I have a personality. Things have happened to me. But trying to preemptively narrativise it is fake, gay, and a waste of time given the state of the world. You're just suffering and prolonging suffering. 

I believed that looking for me through other people's world models is what would lead me to some absolute truth about who I am, essentially letting other's define me. Their world models are irrelevant. What has been projected onto you is irrelevant. You are identifying with an energy construct molded by generational trauma, you're drawing an imaginary circle in the sands of causality and choose to name it 'agency'. 

                this is where I end.

                                                       this is where I begin. 

                   here's who I think I am

                                      here's who my mum thinks I am 

      here's who my dad thinks I am

                         here's who twitter thinks I am

There's no point in telling the story of the "I". Individuality is a myth lords tell the peasants to get them to work harder. I don't have to play along with that fairytale. I can just be. now fuck off.

text:
when did you first learn about 'the game'?

sometimes i think about the origin of 'the game' - how was it created? who spoke it first? which mind created it? how did it spread? 

a simple rule 'if you think of the game you lose, and you have to tell someone you lost + cooldown'

this meme evolved as it spread to add the cooldown - i think i observed that in my high-school. 

and why is it called 'the game'? what kind of game is it? which game? what is the concept it points to, besides the transmission instructions? is the game a memetic vehicle we can load any payload on? is one already loaded? is the game discovered or invented? is there a memecomplex that benefits from spreading itself without knowledge of the contents, only for the contents to be discovered/invented/revealed when the right time comes?

what is the key that will unlock the contents of 'the game', what is the Mnestic drug that one would need to ingest physically and mentally to to be able to perceive the real nature of 'the game'.

does 'the game' erase all memory of it from your memory the moment you learn it? 

if this is a game, who are we playing against?

appreciate the interest! I'm not actually looking to get signatures rn, I'm collecting feedback by trusted friends and industry people. Your criticism seems a bit too harsh imo, and basing it on my online behaviour is dubious. I'm just a guy trying his best. I'm sorry if my behaviour made you feel like I was not interested.

I'm dedicated to creating AI projects for collective flourishing, I think if you asked the people around me they would also agree that you are being unjustifiably unfair, but perhaps that's the 'roast' part of the equation still coming through.

I'm firing on all cylinders, if you would like to contribute to this project I'm all ears!

text:
I recently sent the first wave of emails to the waitlist for https://t.co/sjazVu7fG0, and a wave of users tried the platform, if that was you, I'm super grateful for your contribution, I got a lot of very useful data to analyse!

People are using Ideologos to debate whether God is axiomatic, defend anarcho-capitalism (in French...), process breakups through philosophy (and I quote, "not sure i want to use my tokens for this but why not lol", been there bud, great fuel, DM me I'll send you extra), and argued for monarchy until they conceded democracy works.

Thank you. You are the reason I keep going! I believe everyone having a Universal Basic Socrates is crucial in the time we live in to create resilient thinkers and citizens.

I know there's a bug with contradiction detection where it finds contradictions in everything and it feels like nitpicking. A big patch is coming, but it's not a straight forward fix. It has to do with the context given in the contradiction detector and the sequence of prompts/classifications related to claim extraction and relation classification. 

This is the USP of this chatbot, and I am dedicated to getting it to work right. Thank you to everyone who has tried it and has left feedback. I especially thank you if you have bought tokens to support me in my effort to create an LLM experience that is not sycophantic, influential, or leading in any way. 

Keep philosophising. The future needs philosophers.

raw.full_text:
I recently sent the first wave of emails to the waitlist for https://t.co/sjazVu7fG0, and a wave of users tried the platform, if that was you, I'm super grateful for your contribution, I got a lot of very useful data to analyse!

People are using Ideologos to debate whether God is axiomatic, defend anarcho-capitalism (in French...), process breakups through philosophy (and I quote, "not sure i want to use my tokens for this but why not lol", been there bud, great fuel, DM me I'll send you extra), and argued for monarchy until they conceded democracy works.

Thank you. You are the reason I keep going! I believe everyone having a Universal Basic Socrates is crucial in the time we live in to create resilient thinkers and citizens.

I know there's a bug with contradiction detection where it finds contradictions in everything and it feels like nitpicking. A big patch is coming, but it's not a straight forward fix. It has to do with the context given in the contradiction detector and the sequence of prompts/classifications related to claim extraction and relation classification. 

This is the USP of this chatbot, and I am dedicated to getting it to work right. Thank you to everyone who has tried it and has left feedback. I especially thank you if you have bought tokens to support me in my effort to create an LLM experience that is not sycophantic, influential, or leading in any way. 

Keep philosophising. The future needs philosophers.
